Initiative Name: Power Unit Assembly at 200 units a month
Description: This project is focused on improving Direct Labor Efficiency (DLE) for Power Unit manufacturing operations where current performance exceeds 70% DLE. The objective is to move beyond baseline stabilization and drive incremental efficiency gains through targeted process optimization. Key activities include labor standard validation, work content balancing, reduction of non–value-added activities, improved material flow, and operator utilization optimization. Success will be measured by sustained DLE improvement above current levels, improved throughput, and reduced labor cost per unit without compromising safety, quality, or delivery performance.
Next Action: Monitor for effectiveness. PDCA

Next Action: Order Machine

Description:
This project will upgrade existing blow molding machine controls to ST controllers, modernizing the control architecture and aligning the operation with Industry 4.0 standards. The current control systems are aging and increasingly difficult to maintain, creating elevated risk of downtime, limited data visibility, and reduced reliability. Transitioning to the ST platform will provide a standardized, modern control environment across the blow molding operation.

The upgraded controls will significantly reduce unplanned downtime by improving system stability, diagnostics, and component availability. In addition, the ST platform provides enhanced audit trail capability, cybersecurity protections, and process control, enabling better traceability of machine adjustments and improved compliance with internal operational standards.
Next Action: quote and schedule machine 5/6
Blockers: ST slowness in quoting. Hydraulic splitting package

Description: This project involves implementing a ShopBot CNC cutting system at the Jonesboro facility to replace aging cutting equipment that has become increasingly unreliable and inefficient. The current equipment contributes to unplanned downtime, slower processing speeds, and inconsistent cutting performance, which negatively impacts overall production throughput.
Next Action: Assemble second unit and place into service week of 4/6

Description:
his project focuses on bringing lid production currently being outsourced back into the Jonesboro facility to improve cost control and operational efficiency. Outsourcing has been used to support demand; however, continuing this practice results in avoidable costs related to supplier pricing, lost overhead absorption, and reduced internal labor utilization.

By producing these lids internally, the Jonesboro operation will capture overhead and labor absorption that would otherwise remain unutilized while also eliminating the purchase price variance (PPV) associated with buying finished product from external suppliers. Bringing this volume back in-house allows the facility to better leverage existing equipment and labor capacity while improving overall cost structure.
Next Action: Improve average daily output to 1800

Description:
This project involves clearing and organizing the unused back lot area to enable the space to be subleased to a third party. The lot is currently underutilized and contains excess materials and equipment that limit its productive use. By removing and reorganizing these items, the facility can convert the space into a revenue-generating asset.

Preparing the lot for sublease will require site cleanup, consolidation of stored materials, and establishing defined boundaries for the leased area. Once prepared, the space can be leased to an external tenant, creating a recurring income stream while maintaining operational separation from plant activities.

The expected financial benefit is approximately $20,000 per month in lease revenue, improving overall facility cost absorption and reducing the net operating expense of the site. In addition to the direct financial return, this initiative will improve overall site organization, reduce clutter, and enhance safety and accessibility within the facility grounds.
Next Action: Push for Customer support to relocate assets.
